zh.lifeSummary: Jane Copley於1262年出生在英國越可爾可普利。她與Ralph Stansfeld結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Copley于1262年出生在英国越可爾可普利。她与Ralph Stansfeld结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Copley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name from any of various places called Copley, for example in County Durham, Staffordshire, and Yorkshire, from the Old English personal name Coppa (apparently a byname for a tall man) or from copp ‘hilltop’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Jane Copley was born in 1262, in Copley, Yorkshire, England. She married Ralph Stansfeld. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Copley est née en 1262, à Copley, Everwyck, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Ralph Stansfeld. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Copley wurde 1262 in Copley, Yorkshire, England geboren. Sie hat Ralph Stansfeld geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in England.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Copley è nata il nel 1262 nel Copley, Yorkshire, England. Lei sposato Ralph Stansfeld. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel England.

ja.Copley, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアコプリー
ja.lifeSummary: Jane Copleyは1262年にイギリスヨークシュアイアコプリーで生まれた。Ralph Stansfeldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Copley(은)는 1262년 영국 요르크실르 코플리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Ralph Stansfeld(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Copley nasceu em 1262, em Copley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Ralph Stansfeld. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Copley родилась в 1262 г. в местности копли, еркшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph Stansfeld. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Jane Copley nació en 1262, en Copley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Ralph Stansfeld. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Inglaterra.
